decks.notes: The main focus of this Mono Blue Aggro deck is to efficiently control the board and disrupt opponent's strategies while quickly deploying low-cost creatures to apply pressure. By utilizing disruptive cards like Counterspell and Spellstutter Sprite, along with card draw from Faerie Seer and Of One Mind, the deck aims to maintain tempo and overwhelm the opponent early on. The inclusion of cards like Ninja of the Deep Hours and Brinebarrow Intruder provide additional card advantage and evasive threats. Additionally, Moon-Circuit Hacker can provide a potential finisher with its ability to pump the team.

This deck focuses on aggressive plays using low-cost creatures and spells to deal damage quickly to the opponent. The deck also incorporates cards that can generate card advantage, disrupt the opponent's strategy, and provide recursion for key creatures or spells. The mana base is designed to support the color requirements of the cards in the deck, allowing for smooth and consistent gameplay. Overall, the goal of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggression, disruption, and resilience.

This deck revolves around using control elements such as Counterspell and Journey to Nowhere to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while utilizing Gates to fix mana and generate card advantage. The inclusion of cards like Sacred Cat and Prismatic Strands helps in gaining life and protecting key creatures. The synergy between the Gate lands and cards like Outlaw Medic and Lórien Revealed allows for a powerful combination of defense and utility. By carefully managing resources and leveraging the versatility of the Gates, this deck aims to control the game and outlast the opponent.

This deck focuses on generating mana quickly and efficiently to cast powerful spells such as Big Score and Seething Song. By utilizing Goblin Anarchomancer and Thornscape Familiar to reduce spell costs, along with cards like Pirate's Pillage and Seize the Storm to draw more cards and find key combo pieces, the deck aims to create explosive turns and overwhelm the opponent with card advantage and tempo. The inclusion of cards like Burning Curiosity and Glimpse the Impossible provide additional ways to draw cards and generate value, while Spider Manifestation adds a resilient threat to the board.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures while disrupting their game plan with removal and resource denial tactics. With a mix of efficient creatures and spells that generate value, this deck aims to control the board and apply pressure to force the opponent into a defensive position. By utilizing a combination of sacrifice effects, card draw, and removal, the deck looks to maintain a relentless pace and keep the opponent on the back foot until victory is achieved.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with low-cost, aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. By using cheap draw spells like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Thought Scour, the deck aims to maintain a steady stream of threats while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with Counterspell and Sleep of the Dead. With a mix of evasive creatures like Delver of Secrets and powerful finishers like Cryptic Serpent, the deck aims to close out the game before the opponent can mount a strong defense.

Focused on playing low-cost creatures and disrupting opponents with counterspells, Mono Blue Aggro aims to quickly build a board presence and control the game. Utilizing spells like Brainstorm and Thought Scour to draw cards and fill the graveyard, the deck can cast powerful creatures like Cryptic Serpent and Tolarian Terror for cheap. With a mix of evasion, card draw, and disruption, Mono Blue Aggro can apply pressure early in the game and maintain control throughout.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. By utilizing low-cost creatures and spells like Clockwork Percussionist, Galvanic Blast, and Lightning Bolt, the goal is to whittle down the opponent's life total as fast as possible. The deck also includes cards like Thermo-Alchemist and Reckless Impulse to maintain pressure and provide card advantage. With a heavy emphasis on fast-paced aggression and burn spells, this deck aims to win the game swiftly before the opponent can stabilize.

The main idea of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. By utilizing cards like Counterspell, Snuff Out, and Brainstorm, the deck aims to maintain control of the board and prevent the opponent from executing their game plan effectively. Additionally, cards like Gurmag Angler and Tolarian Terror provide threats that can close out the game once the opponent has been sufficiently disrupted. The deck also includes various card draw spells to ensure that the player has the resources needed to maintain control throughout the game.

The focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with creatures using self-mill cards like Satyr Wayfinder and Stinkweed Imp, then use cards like Dread Return to reanimate powerful creatures like Lotleth Giant or Molten Gatekeeper. The deck also includes cards like Malevolent Rumble for removal and Circle of the Land Druid for ramp. By combining reanimation with powerful creatures, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and win through aggressive plays and strong combo interactions.

The main idea of this Aggro - Affinity deck is to quickly flood the board with artifact creatures and utilize cards like Ichor Wellspring and Drossforge Bridge to draw cards and gain value. Cards like Blood Fountain and Krark-Clan Shaman help to disrupt the opponent's strategy while Galvanic Blast and Reckoner's Bargain provide direct damage to quickly finish off the opponent. The deck also includes some control elements like Duress and Metallic Rebuke to protect your board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with the synergy of artifacts and aggressive creatures.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying artifact creatures and using them to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive attacks. It includes cards that benefit from having artifacts in play, such as Galvanic Blast and Reckoner's Bargain, as well as cards that interact with artifacts like Nihil Spellbomb. The mana base consists of artifact lands to help with fixing and enabling affinity synergies. Overall, the strategy revolves around leveraging the power of affinity to pressure the opponent and close out games swiftly.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent by using aggressive creatures like Kessig Flamebreather and Voldaren Epicure, as well as burn spells like Fiery Temper and Lightning Bolt. By employing cheap spells such as Lava Dart and Fireblast, the deck aims to pressure the opponent's life total early on and finish them off with direct damage spells. Faithless Looting helps to cycle through the deck for more burn spells, while Grab the Prize and Highway Robbery provide additional card advantage to maintain pressure on the opponent.

This deck focuses on aggressively burning down the opponent's life total using efficient creatures like Kessig Flamebreather and Sneaky Snacker, backed up by direct damage spells such as Fiery Temper, Lightning Bolt, and Fireblast. The deck also utilizes cards like Lava Dart and Highway Robbery for additional reach and card advantage. By constantly applying pressure through early creature damage and direct burn spells, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deploy cheap and aggressive creatures, followed by burn spells to finish off the opponent before they can stabilize. Using efficient creatures like Burning-Tree Emissary and Goblin Bushwhacker, along with direct damage spells like Galvanic Blast and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to apply constant pressure on the opponent. Cards like Rally at the Hornburg and Reckless Impulse help maintain card advantage and keep the aggression going. The deck also includes some evasion and protection spells to ensure the creatures can keep attacking.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly and efficiently through cards like Goblin Anarchomancer and Thornscape Familiar to cast powerful spells like Big Score and Pirate's Pillage. These mana-generating spells are used to fuel combos such as Seething Song into Seize the Storm or Burning Curiosity to draw cards and deal damage to the opponent. The deck also utilizes cards like Wrenn's Resolve to protect key pieces and ensure the combo can go off smoothly. By efficiently generating mana and utilizing powerful spells, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly and secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly playing numerous artifacts and sacrificing them for powerful effects. By utilizing low-cost creatures like Mukotai Ambusher and Myr Enforcer along with support cards like Refurbished Familiar and Ichor Wellspring, the deck can overwhelm opponents with a swarm of creatures. Cards like Nihil Spellbomb and Virus Beetle help disrupt opponents' strategies while also creating advantage for the deck. Additionally, the deck has removal options like Cast Down and burn spells like Galvanic Blast to deal with threats. Overall, the deck aims to win the game through aggressive plays and efficient use of its artifacts.

The deck focuses on controlling the board with removal spells like Cast Down and Journey to Nowhere, while also disrupting graveyard strategies with Relic of Progenitus. Squadron Hawk and Sacred Cat provide early game threats, while Outlaw Medic and Dawnbringer Cleric help gain life and stabilize against aggressive decks. Lórien Revealed can be used to draw additional cards and find key pieces like gates to help fix mana. The Gates lands provide essential fixing and allow the deck to consistently cast its spells. This deck aims to outlast opponents and win through attrition.

The focus of this Pauper 1 vs 1 Aggro - Elves deck is to quickly generate mana through creatures like Llanowar Elves and Arbor Elf, while utilizing creatures like Avenging Hunter and Nyxborn Hydra to deal damage to the opponent. Support cards like Quirion Ranger and Timberwatch Elf are included to increase the effectiveness of the elf army. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help refill the hand with creatures. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of powerful elf creatures and deliver a swift victory.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm your opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy using an Elf tribal theme. By ramping up your mana with cards like Priest of Titania and Quirion Ranger, you can quickly play out powerful creatures like Nyxborn Hydra and Timberwatch Elf. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help you refill your hand with more Elves to keep the pressure on. The inclusion of cards like Avenging Hunter and Masked Vandal provide flexibility in dealing with opposing threats. The overall goal is to swarm the board with resilient creatures and outpace your opponent.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through card advantage and tempo manipulation. It aims to stall the opponent's threats with counterspells and removal while generating value through bounce effects like Ephemerate and Ghostly Flicker. The key strategy is to recycle powerful ETB effects from creatures like Mulldrifter and Archaeomancer, drawing cards and outvaluing opponents. The deck also includes lifegain elements like God-Pharaoh's Faithful to help against aggressive decks. By efficiently managing resources and consistently generating advantage, the deck aims to outlast opponents and win through attrition.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through efficient creatures and spells while utilizing the Ephemerate card for added value. It aims to outvalue the opponent by recurring powerful spells with cards like Archaeomancer and Ghostly Flicker. The key strategy is to establish board presence and card advantage through repetition of key spells and abilities, ultimately overwhelming the opponent with card advantage and tempo. By utilizing cards like Snap, Mulldrifter, and Negate, the deck seeks to disrupt the opponent's game plan while advancing its own win condition.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by efficiently recurring key cards and disrupting the opponent's strategies. Using cards like Mulldrifter and Archaeomancer, along with Ephemerate to constantly reuse them, the deck aims to maintain card advantage and tempo. Stonehorn Dignitary can lock down the opponent's combat phase, while counterspells like Prohibit and Negate provide additional disruption. With efficient card draw in Brainstorm and Preordain, the deck looks to outvalue the opponent while preventing them from executing their game plan effectively.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game through the use of defensive creatures and various counterspells, while also utilizing gates to fix mana and gain card advantage. The deck focuses on establishing a board presence with creatures like Sacred Cat and Squadron Hawk, while also using cards like Prismatic Strands and Journey to Nowhere to handle threats. The presence of gates in the deck helps to ensure consistent mana production and card draw, while also providing additional utility through cards like Citadel Gate and Heap Gate.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing efficient creatures, counter spells, and removal spells. The deck aims to stall the opponent's game plan while setting up a strong defense using Gates to provide mana fixing and card draw. The combination of creatures like Sacred Cat and Squadron Hawk with removal spells like Journey to Nowhere and Prismatic Strands creates a strong defensive line, while Counterspell and Dispel can be used to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck's win condition centers around controlling the board and slowly grinding out a victory.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through removal spells and board wipes while utilizing cards that generate value through sacrificing creatures or artifacts. It also aims to ramp up mana through cards like Ichor Wellspring and Lembas in order to cast powerful spells such as Eviscerator's Insight. The deck includes a combination of creature removal, graveyard hate, and creature sacrifice effects to disrupt the opponent's strategy and maintain control of the battlefield. Additionally, the inclusion of Khalni Garden and Bojuka Bog provides extra utility and synergy with the deck's strategies.

This deck focuses on using removal spells and creatures like Troll of Khazad-dûm and Crypt Rats to control the board while generating card advantage with cards like Ichor Wellspring and Thorn of the Black Rose. The deck also includes ways to gain life and resources with cards like Lembas and Campfire, as well as disruption with Nihil Spellbomb and Tithing Blade. The goal is to outlast and outvalue opponents while gradually overwhelming them with threats from the Golgari and Jund color combination.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle utilizing various creatures and spells to put pressure on the opponent. The deck aims to control the board with removal spells and disrupt the opponent's strategy with cards like Duress. Additionally, the deck has a strong synergy with artifact cards like Ichor Wellspring and Drossforge Bridge to generate value and maintain card advantage. Overall, the deck's goal is to quickly build up a threatening board presence and close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The main focus of this Pauper Jund Aggro deck is to quickly generate value by sacrificing creatures and using card draw effects like Ichor Wellspring and Lembas. Utilizing cards like Refurbished Familiar and Writhing Chrysalis, the deck aims to maintain a steady stream of threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan. With removal spells like Cast Down and Toxin Analysis, along with recursion from Pulse of Murasa, the deck can control the board while applying pressure. Additionally, the deck has a land destruction sub-theme with Cleansing Wildfire and artifact synergy with Drossforge Bridge and Makeshift Munitions.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent by utilizing aggressive creatures and burn spells. By efficiently using spells like Lightning Bolt, Lava Dart, Fiery Temper, and Fireblast, along with creatures like Kessig Flamebreather, Sneaky Snacker, and Voldaren Epicure, this deck aims to pressure the opponent's life total. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Guttersnipe and Highway Robbery to further increase the damage output. The deck also includes card draw with Faithless Looting and Grab the Prize to maintain card advantage and keep the pressure on the opponent.

The main concept of this Mono Blue Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with low-cost, evasive creatures like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent. The deck includes a mix of efficient counterspells and draw spells to maintain tempo and control the board. Tolarian Terror adds additional aggression to the deck, while Murmuring Mystic provides token generation for extra pressure. Lórien Revealed is a key card for card advantage, helping to maintain resources throughout the game. Overall, the deck aims to apply early pressure and control the game until victory.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm opponents quickly with a combination of aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. By efficiently utilizing low-cost creatures like Kitchen Imp and Sneaky Snacker alongside powerful burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Fiery Temper, the deck aims to quickly deplete the opponent's life total. Utilizing cards like Alms of the Vein and Vampire's Kiss adds additional reach and lifegain, ensuring the deck can continue applying pressure. Additionally, cards like Faithless Looting provide card advantage, allowing the deck to maintain a steady stream of threats.
